name: Cluster Health Checks
description: Run comprehensive health checks on your Kubernetes cluster to verify node status, component health, and system pod readiness.

steps.title: Check node status
steps.description: Verify all nodes are in Ready state.

steps.title: Check component status
steps.description: Verify core Kubernetes components are healthy.
steps.command: kubectl get componentstatuses 2>/dev/null; kubectl get pods -n kube-system
steps.tip: componentstatuses is deprecated in newer versions. Check kube-system pods instead.

steps.title: Check for pods in error states
steps.description: Find pods that are not running correctly across all namespaces.

steps.title: Check node conditions and pressure
steps.description: Look for nodes experiencing memory, disk, or PID pressure.
steps.command: kubectl get nodes -o json | jq -r '.items[] | .metadata.name + ": " + ([.status.conditions[] | select(.status=="True") | .type] | join(", "))'

steps.title: Verify DNS is working
steps.description: Test that cluster DNS resolves service names correctly.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Cluster Health Checks
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Node Status ==="
kubectl get nodes -o wide

echo ""
echo "=== System Pods ==="
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o custom-columns='NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.phase,RESTARTS:.status.containerStatuses[0].restartCount,NODE:.spec.nodeName'

echo ""
echo "=== Pods Not Running ==="
kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase!=Running,status.phase!=Succeeded 2>/dev/null || echo "All pods healthy"

echo ""
echo "=== Node Conditions ==="
kubectl get nodes -o json | jq -r '.items[] | .metadata.name + ": " + ([.status.conditions[] | select(.status=="True") | .type] | join(", "))'

echo ""
echo "=== Cluster Events (Warnings) ==="
kubectl get events --all-namespaces --field-selector type=Warning --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' 2>/dev/null | tail -10

faq.question: What does NotReady node status mean?
faq.answer: The kubelet on that node is not communicating with the API server. Causes include network issues, kubelet crashes, or resource exhaustion. Check node events and kubelet logs.

faq.question: How often should I run health checks?
faq.answer: Set up automated monitoring with Prometheus and alerting. Manual health checks are useful for incident response and during maintenance windows.
